---
title: "White-dotted Prominent (Nadata gibbosa)"
slug: "nadata-gibbosa"
group: "Insecta"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/623037094/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "White-dotted Prominent"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Animal"
tax_class: "Insecta"
tax_order: "Lepidoptera"
tax_family: "Notodontidae"
categories: ["Animal", "Insecta", "Lepidoptera", "Notodontidae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/623037094/medium.jpg" alt="White-dotted Prominent" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/coreytcallaghan">
coreytcallaghan</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/342163573">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Insecta
-   **Order:** Lepidoptera
-   **Family:** Notodontidae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=82204)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not available
-   **Rarity note:** Uncommon across Florida.

## Natural history notes

Small moth characterized by its smooth tan to brown wings, each marked with a small, pale dot. In Florida, it is found in wooded habitats where its larvae feed on a variety of deciduous trees. Adults are active in the warmer months and are commonly attracted to light at night.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/82204-Nadata-gibbosa)
-   [Local profile](https://explorer.natureserve.org/Taxon/ELEMENT_GLOBAL.2.110853/Nadata_gibbosa)
